narration: The first pass stores the product of everything to the left of each index.
callout: For nums = [1, 2, 3, 4], these are the left-side products.

narration: The second pass moves from the right and multiplies in the product of everything to the right.
decision: Left-side product times right-side product gives everything except self.

narration: Each answer needs both sides because the current value must be left out completely.
callout: One side alone is never enough for this problem.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why would using only the left-side products miss part of the answer?
